rationale: Argo et al. (2008) is cited in the theory development section, not just in a generic background list. The citing paper uses it to support a core premise that prior contact by another person can increase a used product's appeal, contrasted with Argo et al. (2006) for negative contagion. The 2021 paper then builds on that premise by introducing a new receiver-side condition, loneliness, as a driver of preference for previously owned products.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Argo et al. (2008) as prior evidence of positive contagion from desirable others and extends this line by testing when consumers themselves are more motivated to seek symbolic connection through used goods. Specifically, it adds loneliness as a key moderator of interest in previously owned products.
